interviewStages: Behavioral: customer focus, navigating ambiguity, collaboration
codingTopics: OO design: model a business domain such as workflows with triggers and actions
Multi-tenant data structures: per-tenant rate limiting, isolation
Streaming: rolling aggregates over tenant events
Graph and DAG: workflow evaluation, dependency resolution
Search and filters: AND/OR queries over records
systemDesignTopics: Design a real-time collaborative spreadsheet supporting 10K+ rows
Design a formula engine with circular dependency detection and lazy evaluation
Design a workflow automation system (when X happens, do Y)

coding: At Smartsheet, model a workflow engine with triggers, conditions, and actions. Evaluate a workflow DAG and detect cycles.
Implement a per-tenant rate limiter so one customer cannot exhaust shared capacity. Discuss the token-bucket design.
Given a stream of tenant events, maintain rolling per-entity scores with bounded memory.
Implement a filter query over records supporting AND/OR on custom fields. Discuss indexing for a multi-tenant store.
Deduplicate records via fuzzy clustering on name and email. Discuss the matching and complexity.
behavioral: Tell me about a project you are proud of at Smartsheet scale and the hardest tradeoff you made.
Give me an example that shows customer focus, navigating ambiguity, collaboration.
Describe a time you disagreed with a teammate or manager. How did it resolve?
Tell me about a time you took ownership of an ambiguous problem end to end.
systemDesign: Design a real-time collaborative spreadsheet supporting 10K+ rows.
Design a formula engine with circular dependency detection and lazy evaluation.
Design a workflow automation system (when X happens, do Y).
values: Smartsheet succeeds when its customers do. Describe a time you solved for the customer even at a cost to the team.
Tell me about a time you handled a tricky multi-tenant isolation or permissions problem.
How do you balance enterprise customization against product simplicity?
other: How does Smartsheet compare to Asana or Monday.com?

Smartsheet Interview Guide (2026): Work Management Engineering

Updated May 5, 2026 · techinterview.org

⏱ 1 min read

Smartsheet
smartsheet.com ↗

Smartsheet is the spreadsheet-meets-project-management platform used by 90% of the Fortune 100. The interview emphasizes large-scale spreadsheet rendering, formula evaluation, and the engineering challenges of a product where customers can build their own apps with no-code tools.

Process

Recruiter screen → 60-minute coding phone (medium DSA) → onsite virtual: 2 coding, 1 system design, 1 craft deep-dive, 1 behavioral. Cycle: 3–4 weeks.

What they actually ask

- Design a real-time collaborative spreadsheet supporting 10K+ rows

- Design a formula engine with circular dependency detection and lazy evaluation

- Design a workflow automation system (when X happens, do Y)

- Coding: medium DSA, often graph or expression-tree problems

- Behavioral: customer focus, navigating ambiguity, collaboration

Levels and comp (2026)

- SE II: $145K–$180K total

- Senior SE: $210K–$280K

- Staff: $300K–$390K

- Principal: $420K–$550K

Prep priorities

- Be fluent in Java (backend) and TypeScript/React (frontend)

- Understand expression evaluation, AST traversal, and dependency graphs

- Read about virtualized rendering for large grids

Frequently Asked Questions

Is Smartsheet remote-friendly?

Hybrid in Bellevue (HQ), Boston, Edinburgh, and others. Many engineering roles fully remote within US.

How does Smartsheet compare to Asana or Monday.com?

Smartsheet has the strongest enterprise position; Asana is more widely adopted in tech; Monday is design-forward and SMB-friendly. Comp is similar.

Is Smartsheet a "real" tech company?

Yes. Public, growing, and engineering-driven. The product looks unsexy but the underlying systems (formula engine, real-time sync, large-grid rendering) are genuinely difficult.
